title: RIF.3807: REGULATORY COMPLIANCE OFFICER  - SETTORE REFRIGERAZIONE INDUSTRIALE
Cyber Security Compliance Consultant
description:
WHAT WE ’RE LOOKING FOR


### **Join a team of experts dedicated to delivering innovative solutions and driving digital transformation for our clients.**


01##### **How you’ll make a difference**


Support clients in achieving and maintaining compliance with cybersecurity regulations and standards such as NIS2, ISO/IEC 27001, TISAX, and GDPR.


Conduct cybersecurity assessments, GAP analyses, and risk assessments to identify remediation actions and improvement opportunities.


Design, implement, and maintain Information Security Management Systems (ISMS) aligned with international standards and best practices.


Conduct security assessments, GAP Analyses, Business Impact Analyses (BIA), and Risk Assessments to identify critical processes, operational impacts, continuity requirements, and remediation actions.


Analyze business processes, operational dependencies, and critical systems in order to define business continuity parameters such as RTO, RPO, and MTPD.


Manage consulting engagements related to audit readiness, certification support, and regulatory compliance programs.


Act as a Virtual CISO (vCISO), providing strategic guidance on cybersecurity governance, risk management, and organizational security posture.


Develop cybersecurity policies, procedures, standards, and governance documentation.


Deliver training and awareness sessions on cybersecurity, compliance, and security best practices.


Monitor regulatory and compliance developments, advising clients on emerging requirements and their operational impact.


Work on high\-impact cybersecurity and compliance projects.


Collaborate with experienced consultants and international teams.


Grow your expertise across governance, risk, compliance, and cybersecurity domains.


Collaborate with technical and business stakeholders to integrate cybersecurity into organizational processes and digital transformation initiatives. \#LI\-hybrid


02
##### **What we’re looking for**


3–5 years of experience in cybersecurity consulting, compliance, governance, or information security management.


Strong knowledge of cybersecurity frameworks and regulations, including: NIS2, ISO/IEC 27001, TISAX, GDPR.


Proven experience in: Security audits, Risk assessments, GAP analyses, Compliance programs.


Experience in designing and implementing security governance frameworks and compliance processes.


Knowledge of Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery principles and related methodologies (e.g. ISO 22301, ISO/TS 22317\).


Strong analytical, organizational, and documentation skills.


Excellent communication and stakeholder management capabilities.


Ability to work independently and manage projects with varying timelines and priorities.


Client\-oriented mindset with a methodical and detail\-focused approach.


Availability to travel for short\-, medium\-, or long\-term assignments when required.


English proficiency at B2 level or higher.


03
##### **What we would consider a plus**


Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Cybersecurity, Law, or a related field.


Professional certifications such as: ISO/IEC 27001 Lead Auditor, ISO/IEC 27001 Lead Implementer, CISA, CISM, CISSP.


Previous experience as a Virtual CISO, Compliance Manager, or Information Security Manager.


Familiarity with risk management and business continuity methodologies such as: ISO 31000, ISO/IEC 27005, NIST Cybersecurity Framework, ISO 22301


Experience in regulated industries such as automotive, energy, manufacturing, healthcare, or financial services.


Experience with GRC platforms and interactions with certification/accreditation bodies.


Technical understanding of: Cloud security (AWS, Azure, GCP), SIEM and SOC environments, Vulnerability management, Network security architectures, Identity and Access Management (IAM).


What we offer


### **We offer a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your professional growth, personal well\-being, and work\-life balance.**


#### **Welfare Package**


A comprehensive corporate welfare platform, offering a wide range of benefits and healthcare support.


#### **Worklife Kit**


A complete welcome package with all essentials for day\-to\-day productivity, complemented by comprehensive benefits. We offer digital meal vouchers and flexible reimbursement options to support both your work and personal needs.


#### **Empowering People**


Engagement initiatives, team building, and mentoring programs designed to foster integration, knowledge sharing, and meaningful connections.


#### **Open Space, Open Mind**


Modern open\-space offices and collaborative areas that encourage interaction and teamwork.


#### **Career Path**


With our internal Changemaker Path methodology, you'll grow key relational, communication, and leadership skills, that will help you evolve in a way that truly fits you.


#### **Learning \& Development**


Continuous improvement programs, certification opportunities, and incentives to help you grow both your soft and hard skills to unlock your full potential.


### **Our Culture** **\& Values**


At Arsenalia, we believe in creating an environment where innovation thrives, people grow, and everyone can make a meaningful impact.


#### **Redrawing the lines**


We value the courage to take risks and propose ambitious solutions because innovation starts where boundaries are redefined.


#### **In it, together**


Whether it’s a client or a teammate, we show up. With flexibility, commitment, and a can\-do attitude, we support each other and tackle challenges side by side.


#### **Fresh minds and a drop of discovery**


Openness to change it’s part of who we are and how we find opportunities in the unexpected.


#### **Integrity and integration**


We foster a transparent, genuine workplace where authentic relationships fuel collective wellness, celebrating each person's unique individuality in an environment of free expression.

title: Global Product Manager
description:
**Who are we?**

At Vertiv, we’re on a mission to empower the people that will power the future. From a simple swipe to life\-changing medicines, from push notifications to generative AI. We design, manufacture, and service the products and solutions that keep the world connected. With $6\.9 billion in sales, a strong customer base and global reach spanning nearly 70 countries, we are uniquely positioned to deliver greater value to our customers and create new opportunities for our people.  



We are currently seeking a **Global Product Manager** to join our dynamic team in **Piove di Sacco (PD), Italy!**

The Global Product Manager – Chilled Water Systems leads the global strategy, development, and lifecycle management of the Thermal Chilled Water product portfolio. The role is responsible for driving business growth and profitability by aligning global product strategies with regional market needs, overseeing New Product Development, and optimizing the existing portfolio. Working cross\-functionally with engineering, operations, and commercial teams, the position ensures effective product positioning, competitive offerings, and successful go\-to\-market execution worldwide.  



**What kind of work will you be doing?**

The Global Product Manager – Chilled Water Systems is responsible for the end\-to\-end management of the Thermal Chilled Water product portfolio, leading its strategic development, positioning, and execution on a global scale. This role plays a critical part in driving sustainable business growth by ensuring strong alignment between global strategy and regional market needs, while enabling effective execution across all phases of the product lifecycle.


Working in close collaboration with Regional Business Teams, the Global Product Manager ensures that clear, data\-driven product strategies are defined and consistently deployed worldwide. This includes identifying growth opportunities, addressing market gaps, and adapting the portfolio to evolving customer requirements, regulatory environments, and technological trends.


A core responsibility of the role is the ownership of both New Product Development (NPD) and lifecycle management of the existing portfolio. This involves defining product roadmaps, building business cases, and leading cross\-functional development processes to deliver competitive, high\-quality solutions aligned with time\-to\-market, cost, and performance targets. In parallel, the role ensures continuous optimization of the current portfolio through cost reduction initiatives, feature enhancements, rationalization activities, and end\-of\-life strategies, with a constant focus on profitability and market relevance.


The Global Product Manager is accountable for financial performance at portfolio level, including revenue growth, margin expansion. In addition, the role defines and drives global product positioning, value proposition development, pricing frameworks, and commercial enablement. The Global Product Manager supports regional teams with tools, training, and guidance to ensure successful product launches and consistent market messaging worldwide.


Reporting to the Senior Global Product Manager, this role operates in a highly cross\-functional environment, collaborating closely with Engineering, Regional Product Management, Project Management, Operations, Supply Chain, and Sales.  



Responsibilities:  



Product Strategy \& Portfolio Management


* Define and execute the global product strategy aligned with business objectives and market trends
* Develop and maintain a multi\-year product roadmap by segment and application
* Identify product portfolio gaps, overlaps, and rationalization opportunities
Ensure alignment between global direction and regional requirements  
* 

New Product Development (NPD) \& Innovation


* Lead NPD programs from concept to launch, ensuring time, cost, and performance targets
* Build business cases and translate market needs into product requirements
Collaborate with R\&D to develop competitive and future\-ready solutions  
* 

Financial Performance \& Business Ownership


* Own product financials, including revenue, margin, and ROI
* Monitor KPIs and implement actions to improve profitability
Prioritize investments based on strategic and financial impact  
* 

Regional Alignment \& Stakeholder Management


* Align global roadmap with regional needs and growth opportunities
* Act as the key interface between global and regional teams
Support regions with product guidance and strategic direction  
* 

Cross\-Functional Leadership \& Execution


* Drive alignment across engineering, operations, and commercial teams
* Lead cross\-functional initiatives in a matrix organization
Ensure successful product industrialization and execution  
* 

**What will make you successful?**

* Bachelor’s/ Master’s degree in Engineering, Business, or related field.
* 5\+ years of related experience
* Strong technical background with experience in Chiller or CRAH or Air Handling unit’s product.
* Experience in product/portfolio management.
* Strong analytical skills with ability to interpret market and customer trends.
* Excellent stakeholder management and influencing skills.

If **YOU** are the person we are looking for, feel free to apply and let's start drafting your future career in a healthy and growing environment!  



The successful candidate will embrace Vertiv’s Core Principals \& Behaviors to help execute our Strategic Priorities.  



**OUR CORE PRINCIPALS** : Safety. Integrity. Respect. Teamwork. Diversity \& Inclusion.  



**OUR STRATEGIC PRIORITIES**

* Customer Focus
* Operational Excellence
* High\-Performance Culture
* Innovation
Financial Strength  
* 

**OUR BEHAVIORS**

* Own It
* Act With Urgency
* Foster a Customer\-First Mindset
* Think Big and Execute
* Lead by Example
* Drive Continuous Improvement
Learn and Seek Out Development  
* 

\#LI\-DP1  



Vertiv is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. We promote equal opportunities for all with respect to hiring, terms of employment, mobility, training, compensation, and occupational health, without discrimination as to age, race, color, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy status (including childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression (including transgender status or sexual stereotypes), genetic information, citizenship status, national origin, protected veteran status, political affiliation, or disability. If you have a disability and are having difficulty accessing or using this website to apply for a position, you can request help by sending an email to help.join@vertivco.com . If you are interested in applying or learning more about this role, please visit the company’s career page located on Vertiv.com/Careers

title: QUALITY SPECIALIST - PEST MANAGEMENT EXPERT
description:
**LA TUA OPPORTUNITA’:**


l/La Quality Specialist \- PME ha il compito di:

* Monitorare e verificare costantemente la qualità di servizio offerta presso i Clienti della Società.
* Effettuare ispezioni e visite di controllo qualità presso i Clienti in modo da valutare e conseguire il raggiungimento della loro soddisfazione.
* Effettuare visite di ispezione presso i Clienti al fine di consolidare il rapporto con il Cliente e partecipare attivamente alla risoluzione di problematiche di infestazione in essere. Il Quality Specialist deve pianificare mensilmente visite di ispezione compilando il MOD. Quality Checklist in caso di non conformità rilevata e riportare il dettaglio nel sistema ESI web.
* Effettuare almeno due Retrovisite/Audit Day all’anno, finalizzate alla verifica della corretta esecuzione operativa da parte dei tecnici sul campo. Per valutare la conformità degli interventi agli standard qualitativi e operativi aziendali; verificare il rispetto dei requisiti previsti dalla norma UNI EN 16636 per i servizi di Pest Management;
* Visitare i Top Accounts per ispezioni di controllo qualità facendo relazioni/grafici sullo stato del lavoro e sulle infestazioni riscontrate dando suggerimenti per risolvere le problematiche.
* Formare, informare ed addestrare sul campo (attraverso affiancamento) ed in aula (attraverso specifici corsi di formazione) il team operativo (Service Supervisor e Tecnici) e commerciale (Sales) dell’area di competenza per il miglioramento continuo della qualità di servizio offerta (gestione e risoluzione problematiche di infestazione, rispetto delle procedure, miglioramento continuo del rapporto con il Cliente, conformità alla normativa vigente, rispetto delle procedure di lavoro sicuro.
* Formare e informare in aula o da remoto (attraverso specifici corsi di formazione standard o customizzati) i Clienti della propria area di competenza, assicurando la conformità ai requisiti dei discenti e di altri beneficiari, mirando ad accrescere la soddisfazione e lo sviluppo di competenze attraverso l’insegnamento, l’apprendimento o la ricerca (secondo la 21001\).
* Sovrintendere e vigilare sulla osservanza da parte dei Service Supervisor e Tecnici del servizio delle disposizioni aziendali e legislative in materia di salute e sicurezza sul lavoro e di utilizzo dei mezzi di protezione collettivi e dei dispositivi di protezione individuali messi a loro disposizione.
* Collaborare con il Technical Manager per divulgare temi di interesse, informazioni su tutti gli aspetti teorici, pratici e sulla sicurezza relativi all’attività e per informare sulle caratteristiche di nuovi servizi, curare la formazione, la conoscenza e la divulgazione delle istruzioni per la pratica applicazione del servizio di qualità, conforme alle procedure Rentokil e in accordo con le direttive aziendali.
* Assicurare che lo Standard di gestione minimi per trattamenti Entotherm (RMES \- Rentokil Minimum Entotherm Standard) sia venduto o implementato secondo le disposizioni tassative delle norme e legislazione nazionale, Pink Note, Golden Rules, Registro Globale trattamenti Entotherm autorizzati (GAER), Valutazione del rischio Entotherm (ERA), Procedure operative standard (SOP), Procedure di Lavoro Sicuro (PLS), Manuali operatore delle apparecchiature, Checklist\-Moduli Entotherm.

  

Descrizione dell'azienda  

Rentokil Initial è il maggior fornitore al mondo di servizi per le aziende.


Da oltre 90 anni l’azienda si occupa di Pest Control e Hygiene Services. Attiva in oltre 90 Paesi nel mondo – in Europa, Asia, Oceania, America e Africa – conta più di 57\.000 dipendenti e un fatturato annuo di £ 3\.5 miliardi.


Rentokil Initial Italia offre i punti di forza e l'esperienza di una organizzazione multi\-nazionale, pur mantenendo l'agilità e le caratteristiche di una società locale.


L'attività è focalizzata sulla fornitura di un ottimo servizio alla sua vasta gamma di clienti. La filiale italiana opera oggi con due divisioni: Initial Hygiene, specializzata in servizi per l’igiene, che si è ampliata grazie all’acquisizione di CWS\-boco Italia, e Rentokil Pest Control, che nel 2021 si è ampliata grazie all'acquisizione di Gico Systems, dedicata ai servizi per la disinfestazione e monitoraggio degli infestanti.


Lo staff è composto ora da circa 650 dipendenti, che assicurano la copertura del servizio su tutto il territorio nazionale agli oltre 28\.000 clienti.

  

Qualifiche **COMPETENZE E SKILLS RICHIESTE:**


Siamo alla ricerca di un/una brillante candidato/a in possesso di Diploma o Laurea che abbia maturato un’esperienza di almeno 3 anni nel settore Pest Control/Alimentare/GDO/Farmaceutico e che abbia una solida esperienza nella gestione del cliente (aver lavorato nel settore pest management sarà considerato un plus).


Il/la candidato/a ideale dovrebbe possedere:

* Laurea in Biologia/Scienze Agrarie/Entomologia/Scienze e tecnologie Alimentari/Sicurezza ed Igiene Alimentare, Laurea in Medicina Veterinaria o affini;
* Precedente esperienza in aziende preferibilmente del settore Pest Management/Food / GDO/ Ospedaliero/Farmaceutico Precedente esperienza in team gare per la scrittura di capitolati tecnico\-scientifici;
* Regulatory: Conoscenza UNI EN 16636 / Conoscenza Direttiva Biocidi \- Regolamento UE n. 528 del 2012 / Conoscenza della UNI 11381;
* Forte attitudine commerciale e al dialogo continuo con i Clienti;
* Determinazione e ambizione;
* Capacità di analisi;
* Doti organizzative e Orientamento al risultato;
* Orientamento al cliente e alla qualità del servizio;
* Ottima conoscenza degli standard delle certificazioni quali BRC o IFS o 22001 o Marks \& Spencer;
* Disponibilità a frequenti trasferte nella zona sud Italia;
* Completano il profilo la buona conoscenza della lingua inglese e dei principali strumenti informatici.

  

Informazioni aggiuntive  

Il pacchetto retributivo sarà commisurato all'esperienza professionale nel settore.


Completano il pacchetto retributivo:

* Dotazione di pc e cellulare aziendale
* Auto aziendale ad uso promiscuo
* Partecipazione al piano incentivante
* Ticket Restaurant da 8


La ricerca si intende rivolta ai candidati senza alcuna discriminazione di genere, età o provenienza.


Ci teniamo molto alla privacy dei nostri candidati.  

Pertanto, prima di candidarsi è necessario prendere visione della nostra informativa al link di seguito indicato:

title: Bilingual Support Assistant
description:
We are looking for individuals who want to join our team in Vicenza, Italy.  

**Apply now!**  

**Job Opportunity:** **Bilingual Support Assistant (English/Italian)**  

**Location**: Vicenza, Italy  

**Start Date**: Immediate
  

**Schedule**: Full\-time (30 hours/week)  

  

  

  

**Make a Difference Behind the Mission**  

  

Bridge language and cultural gaps as a **Bilingual Support Assistant** at Army Community Services. Your fluency in both English and Italian will help service members, families, and civilian personnel navigate life in Italy with greater confidence and ease. This position offers the unique opportunity to use your language skills to make a meaningful impact during critical life transitions for military families.  

  

This position is an excellent fit for a **military spouse or someone with first\-hand experience supporting military life**, offering both professional fulfillment and scheduling flexibility.  

  

**What You'll Do**  

* Provide bilingual assistance (English and Italian) to ACS customers in person and over the phone
* Perform non\-legal, non\-medical translations for service members, families, and civilian personnel
* Assist customers with completing documentation and forms in both languages
* Support ACS\-hosted community integration workshops with translation services
* Maintain data tracking and reporting through the Client Tracking System (CTS)
* Prepare monthly status reports summarizing bilingual service utilization
* Coordinate translation timelines with customers and COR for complex documents
* Maintain daily customer sign\-in sheets and documentation records
* Provide cultural insights to help facilitate smoother host nation integration

  

**What You Bring**  

* You have strong interpersonal skills and an ability to work with diverse populations
* You are detail\-oriented with strong organizational abilities
* You are cultural aware and sensitive to both American and Italian customs

  

**Qualifications**  

* High School Diploma required
* Minimum 2 years of experience in administrative functions and customer service
* Fluent (Level 4\) proficiency in both English and Italian—reading, writing, and speaking
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in both languages
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and Windows\-based programs

  

**Why You'll Love It Here**  

  

 Flexible 30\-hour work week with scheduling options  

 Unique opportunity to use your bilingual skills daily  

 Direct impact on helping families integrate into Italian culture  

 Work with an international team supporting the military community  

 Variety in daily tasks—no two days are exactly alike  

 Professional environment on a U.S. military installation in Italy  

  

**Ready to Join Us?**  

If you're excited about the opportunity to make a real impact while supporting the military community, we'd love to hear from you.  

  

Apply today and bring your voice, skills, and purpose to a team that values your experience and your drive to make a difference.  

\-

***French Consulting is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements.***

title: Information Technology Specialist (Network)
company: US Department of Defense Education Activity
description:
**Summary**
-----------


**About the Position:**  

This position is with the DoDEA Information Technology Division's Network Operations Center located in Vicenza, Italy  

  

Federal employees in Vicenza, Italy are currently entitled to receive a 20% Post Allowance (COLA) in addition to the base salary. This amount is subject to change at any time.  

  

**This is a Direct Hire Solicitation position.**  

  

The Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) is transitioning to the Department of War Education Activity (DoWEA).


Learn more about this agency


  
**This job is open to**
-----------------------


### **The public**


U.S. Citizens, Nationals or those who owe allegiance to the U.S.
  
**Duties**
----------


* Ensure monitoring and notification services are used on all mission essential network systems defined by Management.
* Analyze network administration Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) on a continuing basis to ensure compliance with established polices and regulations.
* Ensure all IT network systems security requirements related to physical, personnel and information security are accomplished.
* Analyze operating system relationships to resolve issues related to network administration.
* Provide recommendations to respond to security incidents to including contacting users, notify management and security personnel, conducting investigation interviews, and documenting investigation findings.
* Evaluate proposed test plans to assure thorough assessment of elements or operations that may be unique or of special priority.
* Plan, schedule, and execute the installation of new or modified hardware, network operating systems, and applications software.
* Oversee the daily operation and performance monitoring of network systems relating to voice, wireless, firewalls, IP based video distribution and streaming, and data communications.

  
**Requirements**
----------------


### **Conditions of employment**


* Male applicants born after December 31, 1959, must complete a Pre\-Employment Certification Statement for Selective Service Requirement.
* Proof of U.S. Citizenship required.
* Direct deposit of pay is required.
* One year probationary period may be required.
* Appointment subject to a suitability/fitness determination, as determined by a background investigation.
* This position requires a Tier 3 (T3\) investigation.
* You may be required to sign a transportation agreement.
* Must be able to satisfy the requirements of the 26JUL12 DODI 1400\.25 V1230, DoD Civilian Personnel Management System: Employment in Foreign Areas and Employee Return Rights. Visit https://www.esd.whs.mil/DD/DoD\-Issuances/140025/.
* Individuals hired from the Continental United States (CONUS) and their family members (who are US citizens) are required to obtain Official Passports prior to departure to the overseas area.
* Individuals assigned to Italy, England, or Korea, are required to obtain a visa to enter these countries.
* This position requires the incumbent to obtain and maintain a Secret Security Clearance.
* This position requires the incumbent to achieve Foundational Qualification requirements within 9 months of assignment and Residential qualification requirements within 12 months.
* This position requires the incumbent to obtain the Information Assurance Technician (IAT) Level II (Intermediate) certification within 6 months of appointment.

### **Qualifications**


**Who May Apply:** U.S. Citizens  

  

In order to qualify, you must meet the experience requirements described below. Experience refers to paid and unpaid experience, including volunteer work done through National Service programs (e.g., Peace Corps, AmeriCorps) and other organizations (e.g., professional; philanthropic; religious; spiritual; community; student; social). You will receive credit for all qualifying experience, including volunteer experience. Your resume must clearly describe your relevant experience; if qualifying based on education, your transcripts will be required as part of your application.  

  

**Specialized Experience:** One year of specialized experience which includes: Administering and maintaining enterprise\-level network infrastructure (LAN/WAN, WLAN, SD\-WAN, firewalls, and remote access solutions) across geographically dispersed locations to ensure secure, reliable connectivity. Monitoring and managing network performance, availability, and security using enterprise management tools (e.g., SolarWinds, Cisco Catalyst Center, or equivalent) to identify outages, degradation, vulnerabilities, and misconfigurations. Performing advanced Tier II network troubleshooting and incident response, coordinating resolution with internal teams, service providers, and vendors in a Network Operations Center (NOC) or similar enterprise environment. Implementing, validating, and documenting network configuration changes, system upgrades, and operational procedures, including development and maintenance of SOPs and technical documentation in support of IT service management and cybersecurity compliance. This definition of specialized experience is typical of work performed at the next lower grade/level position in the federal service (GS\-11\).  

  

The specialized experience must include, or be supplemented by, information technology related experience (paid or unpaid experience and/or completion of specific, intensive training, as appropriate) which demonstrates each of the four competencies, as defined:  

  

(1\) Attention to Detail \- Is thorough when performing work and conscientious about attending to detail. Examples of IT\-related experience demonstrating this competency include: completing thorough and accurate work independently, even in the most difficult or stressful situations; occasionally reviewing work completed by others;  

  

(2\) Customer Service \- Works with clients and customers (that is, any individuals who use or receive the services or products that your work unit produces, including the general public, individuals who work in the agency, other agencies, or organizations outside the Government) to assess their needs, provide information or assistance, resolve their problems, or satisfy their expectations; knows about available products and services; is committed to providing quality products and services. Examples of IT\-related experience demonstrating this competency include: resolving routine and non\-routine problems, questions, or complaints; developing and maintaining strong, mutually supportive working relationships with customers; conducting evaluation of support to determine quality of services and customer satisfaction, and recommending procedural changes based on customer need or changes in policy and/or regulation;  

  

(3\) Oral Communication \- Expresses information (for example, ideas or facts) to individuals or groups effectively, taking into account the audience and nature of the information (for example, technical, sensitive, controversial); makes clear and convincing oral presentations; listens to others, attends to nonverbal cues, and responds appropriately. Examples of IT\-related experience demonstrating this competency include: convincingly conveying complex information to customers; presenting thoughts that are well\-organized and demonstrating confidence in the facts and ideas; adjusting style when working with individuals with different levels of understanding; using various methods to explain and convey information; and  

  

(4\) Problem Solving \- Identifies problems; determines accuracy and relevance of information; uses sound judgment to generate and evaluate alternatives, and to make recommendations. Examples of IT\-related experience demonstrating this competency include: solving complex or sensitive problems by developing and proposing strategic alternatives; identifying possible conflicts and shared benefits; helping team anticipate problems and identifying and evaluating potential sources of information; providing feedback and coaching to others to help solve problems; engaging appropriate stakeholders when developing solutions in order to understand and incorporate multiple perspectives and needs; evaluating the effectiveness of decisions and adjusting future decisions as appropriate.

title: Local Italy Program Leader (Summer 2026)
description:
**Local Italy Program Leader (Summer 2026\)**


Rustic Pathways is a student travel organization that offers the world's finest experiential education, community service, foreign language, and adventure programs. Our destinations stretch from the white\-sand beaches of Fiji to the rainforest canopies of Costa Rica, the high Atlas mountains to the Great Barrier Reef, and many places in between. We provide superior quality travel and service in some of the most welcoming countries for students around the world. Rustic Pathways demands professionalism and integrity across all of our operations, insists on quality in all aspects of our programs, and places a strong emphasis on the protection, safety, and well\-being of our students and staff. We empower students through innovative and responsible travel experiences to positively impact lives and communities around the world.

**Local Italy Program Leader** at Rustic Pathways

**The Position**


As a Local Program Leader, you live in the country of operation, Italy. Your primary responsibility is the care and well\-being of our students while they travel. You are a primary responder in all escalated situations, incidents, and emergencies. In partnership with your co\-leaders, you’ll collaborate to create a safe and successful program experience for all Rustic Pathways students. You’ll work directly with international leaders, community partners, activity operators, and Rustic Pathways managers. You are a liaison between the program experience and all stakeholders, providing trip updates and managing communication with parents and the global Rustic Pathways team. Expect to spend long days in some of the most welcoming communities on the planet. Community service professionals, customer service experts, globally conscious travelers, logistics lovers, and youth development facilitators are encouraged to apply. Rustic Pathways is committed to increasing diversity in our staff and recognizes the benefit of having a diverse, inclusive work environment. We actively encourage those from equity\-seeking groups to apply. We believe in the value of diverse perspectives and experiences represented by our leaders.


Must be safety\-oriented, with a passion for experiential education. The position requires a lot of physical and emotional stamina.


This is a seasonal position.

**Responsibilities**

* Embody Rustic Pathways’ mission, vision, and values
* Act as a role model and mentor to students
* Protect the safety of students and children by abiding by our zero\-tolerance policy for inappropriate behavior
* Monitor and manage student well\-being
* Follow Rustic Pathways’ protocols while on the program
* Address any student health issues and communicate action plans with parents
* Respond calmly to issues as they arise with a focus on customer service and safety
* Demonstrate responsible leadership and judgment, including cultural sensitivity
* Create positive and inclusive group dynamics
* Ensure students understand the history, culture, and context of the places visited
* Model responsible travel by adhering to Rustic Pathways' sustainability protocols
* Facilitate discussions drawing from your own personal experience and Rustic Pathways' discussion guides
* Serve as the primary communicator with local vendors–including restaurants, transportation providers, and activity partners–using the local language as needed
* Coordinate daily logistics with drivers, guides, and other service providers to ensure smooth program operations
* Support translation and cultural mediation between students, staff, and local partners to enhance cross\-cultural understanding and ensure clear communication

**Job Qualifications**

* **Experience:**
	+ At least 2 years of relevant experience in service\-learning, tourism, outdoor leadership, youth mentorship, customer service, or experiential education.
	+ At least 2 years of experience working with young adults and/or adolescents
	+ At least 6 months of experience living and/or working in an international, multicultural, or cross\-cultural environment
* **Education:**
	+ Bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university OR equivalent work experience
* **Requirements:**
	+ Must be 22 years of age or older before your start date
	+ Demonstrated maturity and leadership skills
	+ Fluency in English and Italian
	+ Basic CPR and First Aid certification from a licensed provider
		- *NOTE: Certifications must ONLY be obtained through classroom or a blended online \+ classroom training.* ***Online\-only certifications are not acceptable.*** *Certificates must be submitted to Rustic Pathways at least three weeks prior to the start of Program Leader training*
* **Working knowledge in the following areas and/or roles:**
	+ Adventure Guiding, Outdoor Education, or Wilderness Therapy
	+ Teaching and/or instruction, specifically middle school or high school, in a multicultural environment
	+ Teaching (any subject) to non\-native English speakers
	+ Leadership Instruction
	+ Customer Service
	+ Volunteering with AmeriCorps, Peace Corps, and/or national service, or comparable positions
	+ Sociology or Anthropology
	+ Social Justice, Immigration, or Migration
	+ Marine Biology, Environmental Studies, Conservation, or Wildlife Biology
	+ Public Health, Global Health, or Water sanitation and hygiene program
	+ Mountaineering or Technical Climbing
	+ Community Service Coordinator
	+ Surf Instructor
	+ Soccer Coach
	+ Camp Counselor
* **Preferred Certifications (including but not limited to):**
	+ Wilderness First Responder
	+ Wilderness EMT
	+ Ocean Lifeguard
	+ Teaching Certifications (ex. TEFL/TESL)
* **Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:**
	+ Appreciation for diversity in cultures and experiences

title: Tecnico Automotive Assistenza Diagnosi e Diagnosi da Remoto
description:
Launch Italy ricerca una figura tecnica da inserire all’interno del proprio reparto assistenza, con focus su supporto diagnostico, assistenza tecnica telefonica e diagnosi da remoto.

La risorsa si occuperà di supportare officine, autoriparatori e clienti professionali nell’utilizzo degli strumenti di diagnosi Launch, fornendo assistenza tecnica qualificata e contribuendo alla risoluzione delle problematiche diagnostiche sui veicoli.

Principali attività

* Gestione delle richieste di assistenza tecnica tramite telefono, ticket e canali digitali.
* Supporto all’utilizzo degli strumenti di diagnosi Launch.
* Assistenza su funzioni diagnostiche, codifiche, reset, calibrazioni e procedure operative.
* Supporto alla diagnosi da remoto tramite piattaforme e strumenti dedicati.
* Analisi preliminare delle problematiche segnalate dalle officine.
* Collaborazione con il team tecnico interno per la gestione dei casi più complessi.
* Aggiornamento e tracciamento delle attività svolte tramite sistema ticketing.

Requisiti richiesti

* Esperienza nel settore automotive, preferibilmente in officina, diagnosi elettronica o assistenza tecnica.
* Conoscenza dei sistemi di diagnosi automotive e delle principali architetture elettroniche veicolo.
* Buona capacità di analisi tecnica e problem solving.
* Buone capacità comunicative e orientamento al cliente.
* Capacità di lavorare in team e gestire più richieste contemporaneamente.
* Buona dimestichezza con strumenti informatici e piattaforme digitali.

Requisiti preferenziali

* Esperienza nell’utilizzo di strumenti di diagnosi multimarca.
* Conoscenza di procedure di codifica, adattamento, reset service, rigenerazioni, calibrazioni ADAS e funzioni speciali.
* Conoscenza della lingua inglese.

Cosa offriamo

* Inserimento in un’azienda internazionale in forte sviluppo.
* Formazione continua sui prodotti Launch e sulle nuove tecnologie automotive.
* Ambiente tecnico dinamico e orientato all’innovazione.
* Possibilità di crescita professionale nel settore della diagnosi automotive avanzata.
* Coinvolgimento in attività legate alla diagnosi da remoto e ai nuovi servizi digitali per officine.

title: Electronic Lead Engineer
description:
**Location:** Italy (preferred: Limana, Milano – also flexible within North of Italy)

 ***Step into Group‑Level Technical Leadership with Epta Group!***


Electronics at EPTA is no longer a component — it is becoming a **strategic pillar**.


As our products evolve towards smarter, connected, and more intelligent commercial refrigeration, EPTA is building a **new Group‑level electronics leadership structure**. We are now looking for an **Electronic Lead Engineer** to play a key role in this transformation.


This is a **newly created, high‑impact role** — a natural next step for a senior electronics engineer who wants to move beyond local projects and **lead electronics at Group level**.

 **Why This Role Is a Career Accelerator**

* **From expert to leader**: move into a true Group technical leadership role
* **Influence without hierarchy**: lead and coordinate senior experts across multiple countries
* **Strategic visibility**: work directly with the Group Innovation \& Technology Director
* **Build something new**: define standards, direction, and ways of working for electronics across EPTA
* **Long‑term impact**: shape how electronics is designed, validated, and industrialized for years to come

 **Your Mission**


You will act as **EPTA’s Group reference for electronics**, coordinating and leveraging the expertise of senior electronics engineers located in different R\&D sites at European level.


Your focus is not just technical excellence — but **harmonization, leadership, and strategic direction**.

 **What You Will Do**

**Group Electronics Leadership**

* Lead the development of electronics across EPTA’s product portfolio
* Coordinate and guide senior hardware, firmware, and embedded software experts across multiple locations (no direct reporting lines)
* Define design guidelines, standards, and best practices at Group level

**Electronics \& Connectivity**

* Lead development and validation of electronic solutions for commercial refrigeration equipment
* Oversee embedded firmware, electronic hardware, controllers, and field communication protocols *(ModBus, LIN, CANbus, MQTT, I²C, etc.)*
* Contribute to architecture for in‑store communication and store‑to‑cloud connectivity
* Support the development of IoT‑enabled solutions integrated with store supervisors and digital platforms

**Quality, Industrialization \& Compliance**

* Oversee PCB schematic and layout activities ensuring manufacturability, reliability, and compliance
* Lead EMC validation, qualification plans, and electronic testing methodologies
* Ensure alignment with Group technical standards and regulatory requirements (e.g. CRA, RED)

**Supplier \& Innovation Interface**

* Support Purchasing in electronics supplier management and cost‑saving initiatives
* Collaborate with Innovation Centre on technology scouting and emerging electronics solutions
* Support Service and Quality teams in field issue resolution and continuous improvement

 **Who We Are Looking For**


This role requires **seniority, credibility, and leadership maturity**.


You are likely someone who:

* Has already been a technical reference within an organization
* Enjoys leading through influence rather than formal authority
* Can challenge experts and suppliers with confidence
* Wants to broaden impact instead of staying limited to one site or product

 **Your Background**

* Master’s degree in Electronic Engineering, Automation Engineering or similar
* **7\+ years of experience** in electronics / embedded systems / product development
* Strong understanding of electronics as a **system**: hardware, firmware, embedded software, physical integration
* Experience in industrialized products (not only prototypes)
* Fluent Italian and English (at least B2/intermediate level)

  

If you are looking for:

* More strategic influence
* Group‑level exposure
* A bridge from senior engineer to technical leader


…this role is built for you.

 **Join EPTA and shape the electronics backbone of tomorrow’s commercial refrigeration.**

title: Legal Counsel
description:
**JOB SUMMARY**

As Legal Counsel, you will report to the Associate General Counsel – Global Legal Procurement and assist others within the Vertiv Global Legal Department. You will assist in providing practical and timely legal support to the Global Procurement and Supply Chain Management functions, focusing on contract drafting, review, and compliance matters under close guidance and mentorship.


**ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS:** (List the major or most important job duties in order of most important to least important.)  



Draft, review, and support negotiation of standard procurement and supply chain contracts, including:


* Master Service Agreements
* Purchase Orders
* Non\-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs)
* Supply Agreements
* Logistics Agreements
* Manufacturing Agreements
* Software\-as\-a\-Service (SaaS) Agreements
* Advise on supplier risk management, including due diligence, contract terms to mitigate risk, and compliance with anti\-bribery and corruption laws (e.g., FCPA).
* Conduct legal research and help prepare summaries on supplier risk, compliance, and regulatory issues.
* Support implementation of global procurement policies and contract management processes.
* Assist with reviewing and maintaining legal templates, playbooks, and contract records.
* Collaborate with internal business teams and external counsel to ensure timely and compliant contract execution.
* Partner with the procurement and supply chain teams to develop and implement legal compliance programs.
* Stay up\-to\-date on relevant laws and regulations impacting global procurement and supply chains.
* Manage outside counsel relationships for specialized legal matters.
* Proactively identify and address potential legal issues related to procurement activities.
* Provide clear and concise legal advice to business partners in a timely manner

**SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE**

* Demonstrated strong broad\-based experience in various areas of law;
* Demonstrated strong experience with commercial transactions, including drafting and negotiating transaction documentation; and
Good understanding of corporate and contract law principles.  
* 

Demonstrated ability to manage multiple tasks, meet deadlines, and communicate effectively in a multicultural environment.


**REPORTING RELATIONSHIPS** **:** (Titles of associates directly supervised.)


The Legal Counsel will report to the Associate General Counsel – Global Legal Procurement.


**INTERACTIONS** **:** (Describe the relationships and degree of contact with internal and external individuals or groups)


The Legal Counsel will interact with our management, and business associates across our corporate and business functions and departments, including but not limited to, manufacturing and operations, procurement and supply chain, IT, engineering, and strategy and business development teams, as well as with myriad of external legal service providers, and other individuals depending on the issue at hand.


**POSITION ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS** **:** (Special conditions required of the job such as travel, driver’s license, physical working conditions.)


**MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS:** (Knowledge, Skills and Abilities Required.)


* Collaboration. Ability to be a good team player by helping others in the legal department and other Vertiv departments, and by being versatile, positive, and diplomatic.
* Client Service. Ability to make positive impressions on clients (internal and external) of the legal department by being polite, responsive, professional and helpful to users of legal services. Lives and displays in actions Vertiv’s values, and provides candid and constructive counsel.
* Communication. Ability to explain and advise clients of the facts and express points of view clearly and logically in written and oral communications. Must have excellent writing and communications skills, as well as practical and creative problem\-solving skills.
* Planning. Ability to prioritize tasks logically and plan work efficiently to maximize the administrative support for legal function available from limited resources.
* Judgment. Ability to discriminate relevant from irrelevant information and significant/material issues from insignificant/immaterial issues.
* Independence. Highly motivated self\-starter, able to accomplish tasks, make routine decisions and handle frequently encountered problems with minimum supervision, action oriented, results driven, sets clear expectations and milestones, reviews progress, acts decisively to solve problems, accountable for consistently meeting or exceeding goals.
* Maintain Confidentiality. Ability/self\-discipline to maintain the confidentiality of client information.

**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS:** (The minimum education, certification, special training and years of experience required to do a satisfactory job.)


* aw degree from an accredited university with strong academic record.
* Member (or eligibility) of a national/state bar.
* 1–3 years of experience in an in\-house or law firm environment with exposure to commercial transactions or procurement matters.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.  
* 

Strong organizational skills, analytical mindset, and attention to detail.


**ACCOUNTABILITIES** **:**

* Deliver high\-quality, timely legal advice and contract support.
* Maintain accurate contract records and documentation.
Support continuous improvement and consistency in procurement legal processes.  
* 

\#LI\-CB1  



Demonstrate learning agility, collaboration, and professionalism in all interactions.
